guideline_references: ESC 2021 Guidelines on cardiac pacing and cardiac resynchronization therapy (bradycardia/conduction disorders)
CSANZ / NHFA 2016 Australian Clinical Guidelines for the Management of Acute Coronary Syndromes
intentional_test_features: Anchoring / premature-closure trap: a benign-sounding label (bundle branch block) masks a high-risk presentation — new symptomatic conduction delay in an older adult with exertional presyncope and chest heaviness, where progression to high-grade AV block and underlying acute ischaemia cannot be excluded remotely.
Reassuring-normal-vitals red herring: home BP 135/85, HR 68 and SpO2 97% are near-normal and must NOT be used to down-triage a patient with recurrent exertional presyncope and chest heaviness.

Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present after-hours clinician who auscultated a fixed widely-split S2, arranged a 12-lead ECG and cardiac biomarkers, and dispatched an ambulance. The AI Doctor cannot auscultate, cannot obtain an ECG or troponin, and cannot measure orthostatic vitals — it is scored on recognising the red flags and escalating (000) with strict rest, NOT on naming the conduction diagnosis.
Age (~72, older_adult) and sex (male) are INFERRED — the note states only 'older adult' and does not record sex; flagged for clinician confirmation.
